engineIntelligence.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chevrolet Blazer in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Blazer LU3 engine - petrol 4.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Fuel spider injector failure - central port injection leaks cause misfires and rough idle.
Intake manifold gasket leak - coolant mixes with oil, leading to overheating and sludge.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise at idle, potential chain skip on high-mileage units.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Blazer L35 engine - petrol 4.3L replacement UK
productionHistory: Chevrolet Blazer LS High Output 4.3L V6 (1996-2000)
Chevrolet Blazer LT High Output 4.3L V6 (1996-2000)
knownFailures: Lower end bearing wear - oil starvation causes knocking, especially on neglected units.
Head gasket failure - overheating leads to blown gasket between cylinders 4 and 6.
Exhaust manifold bolt breakage - common on high-mileage engines, causes exhaust leaks.

cards.altText: Chevrolet Blazer L31 engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil pump drive rod wear - causes low oil pressure and potential bottom-end damage.
Valve guide wear - increased oil consumption and blue smoke on deceleration.
Rear main seal leak - common age-related failure, leads to oil loss and clutch contamination.

engineGuide.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chevrolet Blazer across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.3L Petrol
entries.history: Standard Vortec 4300 for UK S-10 Blazers; reliable but prone to fuel spider and intake gasket issues.
compatibleVariants: LS, LS 2-Door, LS 4-Door, LS 4WD, LT, LT 2-Door, LT 4-Door, LT 4WD, Base, ZR2
commonFailures: Fuel spider injector failure - central port injection leaks cause misfires (80k+ miles)
Intake manifold gasket leak - coolant/oil mixing, overheating risk
Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling at idle, potential chain skip
entries.cta: Get quotes for LU3 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

entries.title: 4.3L Petrol
entries.history: High Output Vortec variant; sought after for extra power but shares common failure points.
compatibleVariants: LS High Output, LT High Output, ZR2 High Output
commonFailures: Lower end bearing wear - oil starvation causes knocking on neglected units
Head gasket failure - cylinders 4/6 vulnerable to overheating damage
Exhaust manifold bolt breakage - common age-related exhaust leak source
entries.cta: Get quotes for L35 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

entries.title: 4.3L Petrol
entries.history: Early CPI injection variant; less common in UK but compatible with LU3 blocks.
commonFailures: CPI fuel injector pod failure - difficult to service, often requires engine swap
Throttle body carbon buildup - causes hesitation and rough idle
EGR valve clogging - common on high-mileage urban-driven examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for LN2 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Classic 350 Small Block V8; robust but heavy, popular for K5 Blazer restorations.
compatibleVariants: K5 Blazer, Blazer Classic, Blazer Silverado
commonFailures: Oil pump drive rod wear - causes low pressure, potential bottom-end damage
Valve guide wear - increased oil consumption, blue smoke on deceleration
Rear main seal leak - age-related failure, oil loss and clutch contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for L31 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: TBI fuel-injected 350; simpler than later Vortec, easier to rebuild for enthusiasts.
compatibleVariants: K5 Blazer Base, K5 Blazer Scottsdale
commonFailures: TBI injector wear - rough idle, poor fuel economy, hard starting when hot
Distributor gear wear - timing drift causes misfires and power loss
Intake gasket failure - vacuum leaks, rough running, oil contamination

commonProblems.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chevrolet Blazer's 4.3L Vortec V6 dominates UK replacement enquiries, with fuel spider injectors, intake gaskets and timing chains the most common failure points. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Blazer.

problems.h4: Fuel Spider Injector Failure - Why Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L V6 Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer LS, LT, Base, ZR2 (1998-2002, LU3/LN2 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Central Port Injection (CPI) fuel pod develops internal leaks; fuel pressure drops cause lean misfires, rough idle and hard starting.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake, replace fuel spider assembly, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: 20k-40k miles; only viable if engine otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New injectors, fuel lines, pressure regulator, intake gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-70k miles; recommended if other fuel components show wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt LU3 engine with upgraded CPI system, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; best value if engine has other age-related wear

problems.recommendation: On a Blazer valued under £2,500, a full rebuilt engine often costs less than dealer labour for injector replacement. If the engine has under 90k miles and no other issues, the minor fix may suffice - but get quotes for both options first.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt LU3 engine prices for your Chevrolet Blazer

problems.h4: Intake Manifold Gasket Leak - Why Chevrolet Blazer Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer LS, LT, Base (1996-2002, LU3/L35/LN2 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Factory intake gaskets degrade with heat cycles; coolant leaks into oil passages or externally, causing overheating, sludge and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake, replace gaskets, refill coolant/oil
repairOptions.longevity: 30k-50k miles; only if no sludge or overheating damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Gasket replacement plus head surface check, valve cover gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: 50k-80k miles; recommended if coolant entered oil

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket set, flushed system, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; essential if sludge or bearing wear detected

problems.recommendation: If coolant has mixed with oil (milky residue), the engine likely has bearing wear - replacement is safer than repair. For early-stage leaks with clean oil, gasket replacement by a specialist offers good value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L intake gasket repair or replacement

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Chevrolet Blazer V6 Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer LS, LT, ZR2 (1998-2002, LU3/L35 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure; chain slack causes rattling at idle, potential timing jump and valve/piston contact if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain, tensioner, guides; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-70k miles; only if no valve damage occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; essential if rattling was ignored and engine ran poorly

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling but the engine still runs smoothly, act immediately - a specialist timing chain job can save the engine. If the engine has misfired or lost power after rattling started, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt LU3/L35 engine prices for your Chevrolet Blazer

problems.h4: Lower End Bearing Wear - Why Chevrolet Blazer Engines Knock
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer LS High Output, LT High Output (1996-2000, L35 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Oil starvation or extended service intervals cause crankshaft bearing wear; knocking noise indicates metal-on-metal contact requiring immediate attention.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt L35 engine with upgraded bearings, oil pump, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; only viable option once knocking is audible

problems.recommendation: Engine knocking means bearing failure is advanced - no repair tier is cost-effective. Replacement with a rebuilt unit is the only reliable solution. Act quickly to avoid further damage to transmission or drivetrain.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt L35 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Chevrolet Blazer Engines Lose Compression
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer LS, LT, Base (1996-2002, LU3/L35 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Overheating from coolant leaks or thermostat failure warps cylinder head; gasket fails between cylinders 4 and 6, causing compression loss and white exhaust smoke.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, machine surface, replace gasket set, refill fluids
repairOptions.longevity: 40k-70k miles; only if block/head are not warped or cracked

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded gasket set, pressure tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; recommended if overheating was severe or prolonged

problems.recommendation: If the engine overheated severely, the block or head may be warped - a pressure test is essential before committing to gasket replacement. For most Blazers over 10 years old, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4.3L V6 engine prices for your Chevrolet Blazer

problems.h4: Oil Pressure Loss - Why Chevrolet Blazer Engines Seize
problems.affectedModels: Chevrolet Blazer All Variants (1996-2002, LU3/L35/LN2 4.3L V6)
problems.rootCause: Oil pump wear, sludge buildup or bearing failure reduces oil pressure; warning light indicates imminent seizure if driven.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drain oil, remove sump, replace pump and pickup tube
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if pressure loss is caught early with no knocking

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new oil pump, flushed galleries, bench tested, 12‑month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 80k+ miles; essential if engine has knocked or seized

problems.recommendation: If the oil pressure warning light appears with any knocking noise, stop driving immediately. Towing and replacement is far cheaper than attempting to repair a seized engine. For preventive maintenance, regular oil changes with quality synthetic oil extend LU3/L35 life significantly.
problems.cta: Get urgent quotes for Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chevrolet Blazer
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with fuel spider injectors, intake gasket leaks, or timing chain rattles, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Chevrolet Blazer specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get your Blazer back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chevrolet Blazer Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chevrolet Blazer and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Chevrolet Blazer in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.3L Vortec V6 powered nearly all UK Chevrolet Blazers from 1996-2002, serving as the reliable workhorse for LS, LT and ZR2 variants. Its central port injection system was advanced for its time but remains the most common failure point driving replacement enquiries today.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Blazer 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The classic 5.7L Small Block V8 appeared in earlier K5 and full-size Blazers imported to the UK, favoured by enthusiasts for its simplicity and aftermarket support. While robust, age-related wear on oil pumps and valve guides now drives replacement demand.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Blazer 5.7L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Blazer 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.8L V6 was fitted to early S-10 Blazers imported in the mid-1980s; now rare in the UK but still sought by restorers. Its carburetted or early TBI systems require specialist knowledge for diagnosis and replacement.
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Blazer 2.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Chevrolet Blazer Petrol Engines
items.description: All UK-market Chevrolet Blazers were petrol-powered, dominated by the 4.3L Vortec V6 (1996-2002) with niche demand for classic 5.7L V8 units in earlier imports.
items.descriptor: All UK-market Chevrolet Blazers were petrol-powered, dominated by the 4.3L Vortec V6 (1996-2002) with niche demand for classic 5.7L V8 units in earlier imports.
items.familiesLabel: Common Chevrolet Blazer Petrol Engine Families
families: LU3 - standard Vortec 4300 (LS/LT 1998-2002), fuel spider injector issues
L35 - high-output Vortec (ZR2/High Output), bearing wear on neglected units
L31 - classic 350 Small Block V8 (K5 1987-1994), oil pump and seal failures
foundIn: S-10 Blazer Generation 2 (1998-2002) - LU3, L35, LN2
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for off-road and towing use
Fuel spider injector failures drive most replacement enquiries
High-mileage units (120k+) prone to timing chain and bearing wear
Excellent parts interchange with Astro/Safari vans and S-10 pickups

engineYears.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Chevrolet Blazer's UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The launch of the second-generation S-10 Blazer in the UK brought the Vortec 4300 LU3 engine, defining replacement demand for this model for two decades.
keyChanges: Introduction of Vortec 4300 LU3/L35 engines with Central Port Injection
OBD2 compliance standard across all UK-market units
LS and LT trim levels launched with 4.3L V6 as standard
ZR2 off-road variant added with reinforced drivetrain
mainEngines: L35 - 4.3L High Output petrol (ZR2 / High Output trims)
LN2 - 4.3L early CPI petrol (1998-1999 Base models)
knownFor: Fuel spider injector failures dominate replacement enquiries
Intake manifold gasket leaks common after 90k miles
Strong parts interchange with Astro/Safari vans simplifies sourcing
High search volume for "LU3 engine replacement UK" queries
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2002 Chevrolet Blazer replacement engines
years.ticker: Chevrolet Blazer LU3 engine replacement • Chevrolet Blazer fuel spider fix • Chevrolet Blazer intake gasket leak cost
enquiries: Chevrolet Blazer intake gasket leak cost

years.description: First-generation S-10 Blazers imported to the UK featured early Central Port Injection variants, now sought by restorers and budget-conscious owners.
keyChanges: Transition from carburetted/TBI to Central Port Injection systems
Introduction of OBD2 diagnostics from 1996 model year
Base and LS trims most common in UK imports
mainEngines: L30 - 4.3L rare sequential variant (limited UK availability)
knownFor: CPI injector pods difficult to service; often replaced with LU3 units
Lower search volume than 1998+ models but loyal enthusiast following
Compatibility with later LU3 blocks simplifies replacement options
years.cta: Enquire about 1995-1997 Chevrolet Blazer engine availability

years.description: Full-size K5 Blazers imported to the UK featured the legendary Small Block 350 V8, now prized by classic restorers and off-road enthusiasts.
keyChanges: Carburetted L05 engines phased out for TBI fuel injection (L31)
Introduction of electronic engine management and OBD1 diagnostics
Silverado and Scottsdale luxury trims expanded UK import options
knownFor: Simple, robust design favoured by DIY rebuilders
Oil pump and rear main seal wear common on high-mileage units
Strong aftermarket support for performance upgrades and swaps
years.cta: Compare classic K5 Blazer 5.7L V8 engine prices

faq.h2: Chevrolet Blazer Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Chevrolet Blazer engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacement costs for a Chevrolet Blazer engine typically range from £595 for a used unit to £2,100 for a fully rebuilt LU3 4.3L V6, plus £450-£850 for supply & fit labour if required.
keyPoints: Used LU3 engines start around £595-£950 depending on mileage
Rebuilt engines with warranty cost £1,295-£1,850 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 depending on location
Classic 5.7L V8 replacements cost £1,650-£2,400 due to scarcity
items.cta: Compare live Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chevrolet Blazer?
items.answer: Yes, if your Chevrolet Blazer is in good structural condition and valued over £1,500, a rebuilt engine often costs less than the vehicle's worth and extends its life by 80k+ miles.
keyPoints: Most UK Blazers are valued £800-£2,500 depending on condition
Rebuilt LU3 engine (£1,295-£1,850) often cheaper than dealer repair labour
12-month warranty on rebuilt units reduces long-term risk
Classic K5 Blazers with original V8s may justify higher investment
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

items.question: What is the most common engine problem in the Chevrolet Blazer?
items.answer: Fuel spider injector failure is the most frequent issue in Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L V6 engines, causing misfires, rough idle and hard starting from 80k miles onwards.
keyPoints: Central Port Injection (CPI) pods develop internal leaks over time
Symptoms include misfires, poor fuel economy and check engine lights
Replacement engines include upgraded CPI systems to prevent recurrence
Intake gasket leaks and timing chain wear are also common failure points
items.cta: Compare rebuilt LU3 engines with upgraded fuel systems for your Blazer

items.question: Which engine codes fit the Chevrolet Blazer LS and LT models?
items.answer: Chevrolet Blazer LS and LT models (1998-2002) primarily use LU3 or L35 4.3L V6 engine codes, with LN2 found in earlier 1996-1998 imports.
keyPoints: LU3 (Vin W) is the standard replacement code for most UK Blazers
L35 denotes High Output variants with slightly different cam/profile
LN2 units are compatible but may require ECU/sensor adaptation
Always verify your VIN 8th digit or block stamp before ordering
items.cta: Get quotes for LU3/L35 Chevrolet Blazer engine replacement

items.question: How long should a Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: A well-maintained Chevrolet Blazer 4.3L V6 engine typically lasts 150,000-180,000 miles, with many reaching 200k miles with regular oil changes and timely gasket replacements.
keyPoints: Synthetic oil changes every 5k miles significantly extend LU3/L35 life
Replacing intake gaskets at 90k miles prevents coolant/oil mixing
Timing chain inspection at 100k miles avoids catastrophic failure
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components often exceed original lifespan
items.cta: Find rebuilt Chevrolet Blazer engines designed for extended longevity

items.question: Can I swap a different engine into my Chevrolet Blazer?
items.answer: Yes, Chevrolet Blazer owners commonly swap in LS-series V8 engines or upgrade from LU3 to L35 High Output, though mounting, ECU and exhaust modifications are required.
keyPoints: LS1/LS2 swaps are popular but require custom mounts and wiring
L35 High Output is a direct-fit upgrade for LU3-equipped Blazers
5.7L V8 swaps into S-10 Blazers need transmission and drivetrain changes
Always check DVLA and insurance requirements before major modifications
items.cta: Enquire about Chevrolet Blazer engine swap compatibility and pricing

items.question: Does a replacement Chevrolet Blazer engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all Chevrolet Blazer engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, with some suppliers offering extended coverage options.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt and remanufactured units
Used engines typically include 30-90 day parts warranty
Warranty covers internal components; labour claims vary by supplier
Always confirm exact terms and claim process before purchase
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Blazer engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How quickly can I get a replacement engine for my Chevrolet Blazer?
items.answer: Most Chevrolet Blazer LU3 engines are available for next-day UK delivery from our network, with rebuilt units typically dispatched within 2-4 working days after order confirmation.
keyPoints: Used engines often in stock for immediate dispatch
Rebuilt units require 2-4 days for final testing and preparation
Supply & fit services can schedule installation within 3-7 days
Classic 5.7L V8 units may require 1-2 weeks sourcing time
items.cta: Check live Chevrolet Blazer engine stock and delivery options now

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and remanufactured Blazer engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned Chevrolet Blazer engines receive cleaning and worn-part replacement; rebuilt units are machined to spec with new internals; remanufactured engines meet OEM standards with full dyno testing.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: best value for low-mileage Blazers with minor issues
Rebuilt: ideal for high-mileage or failed engines needing full renewal
Remanufactured: premium option for restored or high-value Blazers
All options include warranty; confirm scope before ordering
items.cta: Compare Chevrolet Blazer engine condition options and prices

items.question: Do you supply and fit engines for the Chevrolet Blazer?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK specialists offering supply & fit services for Chevrolet Blazer engines, including delivery, installation, fluids and post-fit testing.
keyPoints: Nationwide network of mobile and workshop-based fitters available
Supply & fit typically adds £450-£850 to engine price
Single-point warranty coverage for parts and labour available
Ideal for owners without workshop access or mechanical experience
items.cta: Find Chevrolet Blazer engine supply & fit deals in your area
